story.iWant: composed services to have proper lifecycle management
story.soThat: resources are allocated and cleaned up correctly in service hierarchies
story.tasks: - [ ] Task 1: Implement ServiceLifecycleInterface with core lifecycle methods (AC: 1) - [ ] Define ServiceLifecycleInterface with Start(), Stop(), HealthCheck() methods - [ ] Create lifecycle state enumeration (Created, Starting, Running, Stopping, Stopped, Failed) - [ ] Implement lifecycle event system with proper event propagation through service hierarchy - [ ] Add lifecycle timeout handling for start/stop operations with configurable timeouts - [ ] Task 2: Implement dependency-aware startup and shutdown ordering (AC: 2) - [ ] Create dependency graph analysis from service composition to determine startup/shutdown ordering - [ ] Implement topological sort for dependency-aware service ordering - [ ] Add parallel startup/shutdown where dependencies allow for performance optimization - [ ] Handle circular dependency detection and provide clear error messages - [ ] Task 3: Implement comprehensive health checking and status aggregation (AC: 3) - [ ] Create health status enumeration (Healthy, Degraded, Unhealthy, Unknown) - [ ] Implement health check aggregation with configurable policies (all-healthy, majority-healthy, critical-services-healthy) - [ ] Add health check caching and periodic refresh to minimize performance impact - [ ] Support deep health checks that verify dependency health propagation - [ ] Task 4: Implement graceful degradation and failure isolation (AC: 4) - [ ] Create service isolation mechanisms to prevent failure cascades - [ ] Implement circuit breaker patterns for failed services with automatic recovery - [ ] Add partial service availability patterns enabling continued operation with reduced functionality - [ ] Support graceful degradation policies defining which services are critical vs optional - [ ] Task 5: Implement lifecycle hooks and custom logic support (AC: 5) - [ ] Create lifecycle hook interface (BeforeStart, AfterStart, BeforeStop, AfterStop) - [ ] Implement hook registration and execution with proper error handling - [ ] Add hook timeout handling and failure policies - [ ] Support hook chaining and conditional execution based on service state - [ ] Task 6: Implement service recovery patterns and failure handling (AC: 6) - [ ] Create automatic service recovery mechanisms for transient failures - [ ] Implement configurable retry policies with exponential backoff - [ ] Add service replacement patterns for permanent failures - [ ] Support hot-swapping of services in composition hierarchy for zero-downtime updates

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. **Lifecycle Event Propagation**: Lifecycle events (start, stop, health check) propagate correctly through the entire service composition hierarchy 2. **Dependency-Aware Ordering**: Services start in correct dependency order (dependencies before dependents) and stop in reverse order (dependents before dependencies) 3. **Health Status Aggregation**: Health checks aggregate status from all composed services with proper failure handling and status reporting 4. **Graceful Degradation**: Partial failures are handled gracefully allowing the system to continue with available services while properly isolating failed components 5. **Lifecycle Hook Support**: Custom initialization and cleanup logic can be attached through lifecycle hooks with proper execution ordering and error handling 6. **Service Recovery Patterns**: Transient service failures are handled with automatic recovery mechanisms and configurable retry policies

artifact.title: Product Requirements Document
artifact.snippet: FR20: Service lifecycle management supports proper dependency teardown. Framework provides robust lifecycle patterns for service hierarchies.

artifact.title: Architecture Document
artifact.snippet: Decision 3 implements decorator pattern with middleware pipeline enabling lifecycle management coordination across service composition hierarchies.

artifact.title: Epic 3 Technical Specification
artifact.snippet: LifecycleManager provides dependency-aware service startup/shutdown with health aggregation, graceful degradation, and recovery patterns for service compositions.

artifact.title: Epic Breakdown
artifact.snippet: Implement Service Lifecycle Management with dependency-aware ordering, health aggregation, graceful degradation, and lifecycle hooks for service hierarchies.

artifact.title: Previous Story - Composition Utilities
artifact.snippet: Composition utilities provide foundation for lifecycle coordination with sub-microsecond performance. ServiceChain, ServiceProxy, ServiceBranch patterns require coordinated lifecycle management.

artifact.reason: Existing lifecycle management infrastructure with priority ordering, state management, and health monitoring integration
Comprehensive test patterns for lifecycle management including priority ordering, start/stop cycles, and container integration
Core composition interface that lifecycle management must integrate with for service hierarchy coordination
Test patterns for service composition that lifecycle management needs to support and coordinate
Interface compliance patterns that lifecycle management interfaces must follow for consistency

story-context.constraints: - Epic 3 Integration: Lifecycle management must coordinate with ServiceChain, ServiceProxy, ServiceBranch, ServiceMerger patterns from composition utilities - Performance Targets: Lifecycle operations < 100ms for simple compositions, < 1s for complex hierarchies, sub-millisecond overhead for simple operations - Interface Compliance: All lifecycle interfaces must preserve EndorServiceInterface and EndorHybridServiceInterface contracts - Dependency Injection: Lifecycle management must integrate with DI container for dependency graph analysis and startup/shutdown ordering - Zero Overhead: Abstractions must have zero runtime performance penalty when not using lifecycle features - Memory Optimization: Lifecycle patterns must minimize memory allocation through shared dependency integration - Health Monitoring: Health checks must be optimized with caching and periodic refresh to minimize performance impact - Failure Isolation: Circuit breaker patterns and graceful degradation must prevent cascade failures - Type Safety: Generic type constraints and interface validation patterns must maintain compile-time safety

interface.name: ServiceLifecycleInterface
interface.signature: type ServiceLifecycleInterface interface { Start(ctx context.Context) error Stop(ctx context.Context) error HealthCheck(ctx context.Context) HealthStatus GetState() ServiceState AddHook(hook LifecycleHook) error }

interface.name: LifecycleManager
interface.signature: type LifecycleManager interface { RegisterService(service ServiceLifecycleInterface) error StartAll(ctx context.Context) error StopAll(ctx context.Context) error GetHealth() CompositeHealthStatus GetDependencyGraph() DependencyGraph }

interface.name: LifecycleHook
HealthStatus

tests.standards: Testing standards follow patterns established in Epic 1-2: Unit tests with mocked dependencies using testutils package, integration tests with real dependencies, lifecycle-specific test patterns for startup/shutdown ordering, health aggregation validation, and failure recovery testing. All tests must use build tags for selective execution.
tests.locations: sdk/lifecycle/*_test.go for lifecycle-specific tests, sdk/di/lifecycle_test.go for DI integration, sdk/composition/composition_test.go for composition integration patterns, test/services/ for integration tests

ideas.idea: Test lifecycle event propagation through ServiceChain and ServiceBranch compositions with mock services
Test dependency-aware ordering with circular dependency detection and topological sort validation
Test health aggregation policies (all-healthy, majority-healthy, critical-services) with mixed service states
Test graceful degradation when embedded services fail, verifying parent service continues with reduced functionality
Test lifecycle hooks execution order and error handling with chained hooks and timeout scenarios
Test service recovery patterns with automatic restart, exponential backoff, and hot-swapping capabilities
